tasks.title: Design Article Firestore Schema
tasks.description: Create DB_SCHEMA.md documenting the Firestore schema for articles collection with all required fields including thumbnailURL, createdAt, and isUserArticle flag
tasks.details:
Create `backend/docs/DB_SCHEMA.md` with schema definition:

```
articles/{docId}
├── id: string (auto-generated)
├── author: string (required)
├── title: string (required)
├── description: string
├── url: string?
├── publishedAt: timestamp (required)
├── content: string (required)
├── thumbnailURL: string (Cloud Storage path: media/articles/{filename})
├── createdAt: timestamp (required)
└── isUserArticle: boolean (true for user uploads)
```
Include field types, descriptions, nullability, and validation rules. Reference Cloud Storage path format exactly as `media/articles/{uuid}.{ext}`
tasks.testStrategy: Verify file exists with all required fields documented. Manually validate schema matches ArticleEntity extension requirements. Check thumbnailURL path format references correct Storage folder structure.

Fallback to createdAt if publishedAt missing. Return available data + error metadata on partial failure. Add isUserArticle flag to ArticleEntity.

tasks.title: Update Firestore Security Rules for Ownership Checks on Create and Update
tasks.description: Update `backend/firestore.rules` to enforce userId ownership on both `create` and `update` operations for the `articles` collection, ensuring only the authenticated author can create articles with their own userId and only edit their own documents.
tasks.details:
Open `backend/firestore.rules` and modify the `articles/{docId}` rules:

1. **Create rule**: Change from allowing any authenticated user to requiring `request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`. This prevents userId spoofing on upload. The client already sets `userId` to `currentUser.uid` in `ArticleUploadCubit`, so no client change is needed.

2. **Update rule**: Add `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` to ensure only the document owner can update. Also add `request.resource.data.userId == resource.data.userId` to prevent changing the userId field. Additionally enforce immutability of `createdAt` and `isUserArticle` fields:
   - `request.resource.data.createdAt == resource.data.createdAt`
   - `request.resource.data.isUserArticle == resource.data.isUserArticle`

3. **Delete rule**: Already checks `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` — no change needed.

Pseudo-code for rules:
```
match /articles/{docId} {
  allow read: if true;
  allow create: if request.auth != null
    && request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
    && request.resource.data.keys().hasAll(['title', 'content', 'userId', 'createdAt', 'isUserArticle']);
  allow update: if request.auth != null
    && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
    && request.resource.data.userId == resource.data.userId
    && request.resource.data.createdAt == resource.data.createdAt
    && request.resource.data.isUserArticle == resource.data.isUserArticle;
  allow delete: if request.auth != null
    && resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid;
}
```

Deploy rules using `firebase deploy --only firestore:rules`. Test with the Firebase emulator suite before deploying to production.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Use Firebase Emulator Suite to write integration tests for the security rules.
2. Test that an authenticated user can create an article with their own userId.
3. Test that an authenticated user CANNOT create an article with a different userId.
4. Test that the article owner can update mutable fields (title, description, content, thumbnail).
5. Test that a non-owner authenticated user CANNOT update another user's article.
6. Test that immutable fields (userId, createdAt, isUserArticle) cannot be changed on update.
7. Test that the existing delete ownership check still works.
8. E2E test: verify the existing upload flow still works after rule deployment.
